# Oregon Executive-Branch Knowledge Repository (DAS pilot)

> Curated, NON-AUTHORITATIVE copies of the statutes, administrative rules,
> executive orders, policies, and standards governing Oregon executive-branch agencies,
> starting with the Department of Administrative Services (DAS). Every document carries
> provenance frontmatter and links to its official source — always verify there. The
> FULL VERBATIM TEXT of every state-authored document is included in-repo under each
> file's '## Full text' section (no external fetch needed to quote policy language);
> third-party standards are summarized with links to official sources only. Agent
> rules live in AGENTS.md.

## Statutes (ORS)

- [Statutes index](statutes/_index.md): 1071 ORS sections, full text each — complete
  chapters 183 (Administrative Procedures Act), 184 (Executive Branch Departments incl.
  DAS), 192 (Public Records; Meetings), 240 (State Personnel Relations), 276 (Public
  Facilities), 276A (Information Technology), 278 (Insurance for Public Bodies), 279A/B/C
  (Public Contracting — General/Procurements/Improvements), 282 (Public Printing), 283
  (Interagency Services), 291 (State Financial Administration), 292 (Salaries & Expenses
  of State Officers/Employees), 293 (Administration of Public Funds), 2025 Edition.
  Consult for the legal authority behind any rule or policy.
- [ORS 276A.300 — Information systems security in executive department](statutes/ors-276a.300.md):
  the State CIO's authority over executive-branch information security; the statutory
  root of the whole security stack.
- [ORS 276A.303 — Security for SoS, Treasurer, AG](statutes/ors-276a.303.md): why those
  offices are excluded from DAS security policies.
- [ORS 276A.306 — Security incidents and assessments](statutes/ors-276a.306.md):
  definitions grounding incident response and CSS assessments.

## Administrative Rules (OAR)

- [Rules index](rules/_index.md): 489 rules, full text each — every division of
  chapter 105 (Chief Human Resources Office), chapter 122 (Chief Financial Office),
  chapter 125 (DAS: procurement 246-249, purchasing, surplus, facilities, payroll,
  records, risk, fleet, more), and chapter 128 (State CIO: broadband, covered vendors,
  information security 030, geographic information, e-signatures). Old "OAR 125-800"
  citations resolve to 128-030.
- [OAR 128-030-0020 — State Information Security](rules/128/030/oar-128-030-0020.md):
  DAS as primary point of accountability; agency duties. Also
  [128-030-0005 (purpose)](rules/128/030/oar-128-030-0005.md) and
  [128-030-0010 (definitions)](rules/128/030/oar-128-030-0010.md).

## Executive Orders

- [Executive orders index](executive-orders/_index.md): none ingested yet.

## DAS Policies

- [DAS policies index](agencies/das/policies/_index.md): 133 policies/procedures
  mirroring the DAS policies listing of record (14 categories: Facilities, General,
  Procurement, IT Assets/General/Security, and the full HR corpus — classification &
  compensation, position management, filling positions, workforce management, employee
  leave, discipline & discharge, rulemaking).
- [DAS 107-004-052 — Cyber and Information Security](agencies/das/policies/das-107-004-052.md):
  the statewide security program policy — roles (State CISO, agency heads, users),
  enterprise security baseline, CSS assessments, offshoring prohibition. Companion:
  [107-004-052_PR procedure](agencies/das/procedures/das-107-004-052_pr.md).
- Security cluster: [107-004-050 asset classification](agencies/das/policies/das-107-004-050.md) ·
  [107-004-110 acceptable use](agencies/das/policies/das-107-004-110.md) ·
  [107-004-120 incident response](agencies/das/policies/das-107-004-120.md) ·
  [107-004-140 privileged access](agencies/das/policies/das-107-004-140.md) ·
  [107-004-150 cloud/hosted](agencies/das/policies/das-107-004-150.md) ·
  [107-004-160 data governance](agencies/das/policies/das-107-004-160.md) ·
  [107-004-010 asset inventory](agencies/das/policies/das-107-004-010.md) ·
  [107-004-051 removable media](agencies/das/policies/das-107-004-051.md) ·
  [107-004-100 transporting assets](agencies/das/policies/das-107-004-100.md)
- [DAS 107-001-020 — Public Records Management](agencies/das/policies/das-107-001-020.md)
- Facilities/asset management: [107-011-010 energy & conservation](agencies/das/policies/das-107-011-010.md) ·
  [107-011-050_PR e-waste disposal](agencies/das/procedures/das-107-011-050_pr.md) ·
  [107-011-100 office space design](agencies/das/policies/das-107-011-100.md) ·
  [107-011-115 office siting](agencies/das/policies/das-107-011-115.md) ·
  [107-011-150 service animals](agencies/das/policies/das-107-011-150.md) ·
  [107-011-310 Capitol Mall grounds](agencies/das/policies/das-107-011-310.md) ·
  [107-011-330 vending services](agencies/das/policies/das-107-011-330.md)
- HR: [50-000-01 drug-free workplace](agencies/das/policies/das-50-000-01.md) ·
  [50-010-01 discrimination/harassment](agencies/das/policies/das-50-010-01.md) ·
  [50-010-02 workplace violence](agencies/das/policies/das-50-010-02.md) ·
  [50-010-03 professional conduct](agencies/das/policies/das-50-010-03.md) ·
  [50-010-05 weapons](agencies/das/policies/das-50-010-05.md) ·
  [50-010-06 health & wellness](agencies/das/policies/das-50-010-06.md) ·
  [50-020-10 ADA/accommodation](agencies/das/policies/das-50-020-10.md) ·
  [50-035-01 performance management](agencies/das/policies/das-50-035-01.md) ·
  [50-050-01 remote work](agencies/das/policies/das-50-050-01.md) ·
  [50-090-01 improper conduct reporting](agencies/das/policies/das-50-090-01.md)

## DAS Procedures

- [DAS procedures index](agencies/das/procedures/_index.md): 14 procedures
  (`doc_type: procedure`, filenames ending `_pr.md`) that implement DAS statewide
  policies — split out from DAS Policies so procedures aren't mixed in with policies.
  A procedure's policy is its number minus the `_PR` suffix (e.g. 107-004-052_PR
  implements 107-004-052).

## DAS Accounting Manual (OAM)

- [OAM index](agencies/das/accounting-manual/_index.md): 173 documents mirroring the OAM
  listing of record — 16 chapters (01 Introduction, 05 R*STARS, 10 Internal Control, 15
  Accounting & Financial Reporting, 20 Budgetary Accounting, 25 Management Accounting,
  30 Federal Compliance, 35 Accounts Receivable, 40 Travel, 45 Payroll, 50 Tax Issues,
  55 Other Programs/SPOTS, 60 Chart of Accounts, 65 Glossary, 70 Agency Lists, 75
  Forms). Older documents come as split `.po` policy / `.pr` procedure pairs. 12 files
  are pending drafts not in the listing of record (status `proposed`/`draft`, clearly
  annotated) — always check frontmatter `status` before treating a document as current.

## DAS Standards (EIS/CSS)

- [Standards index](agencies/das/standards/_index.md): the non-policy documents policies
  require agencies to uphold, issued under ORS 276A.300.
- [Statewide IT Control Standards (Jan 2024)](agencies/das/standards/eis-css-itcs.md):
  the baseline control catalog agencies must meet (NIST SP 800-53 r5 aligned); per-family
  inventories in [eis-css-it-control-standards/](agencies/das/standards/eis-css-it-control-standards/_index.md).
- [Statewide Information Security Plan (2018)](agencies/das/standards/eis-css-secplan.md)
  and [2023 Security Program Plan](agencies/das/standards/eis-css-program-plan-2023.md).

## External References

- [External references index](external-references/_index.md): summaries + links only.
- [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](external-references/nist-sp-800-53-r5.md): the control
  framework behind the statewide standards.
